text: Diabetic nephropathy (DN) is one of the serious complications of diabetes, which seriously affects the quality of life of patients. Research has shown that rhein can be used to treat DN. This study aims to investigate the role and mechanism of rhein in treating DN. Results showed that rhein can significantly improve the survival rate of cells and decrease the reactive oxygen species (ROS) level in high glucose-induced (HG) podocyte injury model and streptozocin-induced (STZ) DN mouse model. Rhein intervention can also inhibit the expression of Ras-related C3 botulinum toxin substrate 1 (Rac1), NADPH Oxidase 1 (NOX1), and β-catenin. In addition, the content of glutathione peroxidase 4 (GPX4) and the distribution of α-smooth muscle actin (α-SMA), Nephrin, and Podocin were improved through rhein intervention, as confirmed by immunofluorescence (IF) staining, and rhein intervention could further alleviate the renal morphological changes and tubulointerstitial fibrosis, as observed by hematoxylin-eosin (HE) staining and Masson staining, and improved the content of various biochemical indicators, as confirmed by kits, and the contents of α-SMA and Nephrin in kidney tissues were also improved, as confirmed by immunohistochemistry (IHC) staining. Gene silencing of Rac1 (si-Rac1) inhibited the accumulation of ROS and malondialdehyde (MDA) and Fe2+, and also inhibited the expression of α-SMA, NOX1, β-catenin, and other inflammatory factors. Conversely, the Rac1 overexpression vector (oe-Rac1) was used to inject HG-treated podocytes to explore the function and observed that oe-Rac1 inhibited the expression of SOD, glutathione (GSH) and GPX4, and down-regulated the expression of Recombinant Solute Carrier Family 7, Member 11 (SLC7A11) and Nephrin in HG-treated podocytes. Meanwhile, the Rac1 Lentivirus (LV-Rac1) injection of DN mice enhanced the accumulation of ROS and MDA and Fe2+, and increased the expression of RAC1, NOX1, β-catenin, TFR1, ACSL4, and α-SMA. In summary, rhein could inhibit ferroptosis and EMT of DN through regulation of the Rac1/NOX1/β-catenin signaling pathway.
explanation: Its use of domain-specific biomarkers and a coherent experimental narrative is consistent with real scientific writing, making AI authorship plausible but not certain. Some phrasing appears slightly generic and repetitive, which can be a sign of AI-generated text.
